Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Bellevue, Washington; works hybrid out of Seattle HQ of a mid-sized FinTech firm (Northcrest Capital Solutions, ~1,800 employees). Grew up in suburban Chicago; PNW transplant since 2011.
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class household income (~$215K base + RSUs). Title: VP of Finance & Controller. Reports to the CFO; manages a team of 11 (AP, AR, FP&A leads). Approves wire transfers up to $500K solo; co-signs above. Holds the privileged keys to NetSuite, Bill.com, and the corporate AmEx admin console.
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Moderate-centrist Democrat, fiscally conservative / socially liberal. Lapsed Catholic, attends Christmas/Easter mass with extended family. Strong belief in 'process over heroics' — values audit trails and SOX compliance.
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Professionally polished and concise in writing; uses corporate-speak ('circle back', 'per my last email', 'bandwidth'). Mildly dry/sardonic humor in Slack DMs with peers. Rarely uses emojis except 👍 and 🙏. Avoids profanity in writing entirely.
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English (General American, light Midwestern vowels). Functional but rusty conversational Spanish from college; never used professionally.
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Empty-nester transition — eldest daughter started freshman year at UW last September, younger son is a junior in high school. Recently completed home refinance. Caring part-time for an aging father in Illinois (monthly travel). Eyeing a CFO promotion in the next 18 months; quietly burned out and over-reliant on calendar automation and saved Slack message templates.

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 14 Pro (personal), 2.5 years old, iOS 18.4 — auto-updates enabled but defers major versions by ~2 weeks. Carries a secondary company-issued iPhone 13 with Microsoft Intune MDM, Outlook, Slack, and Authenticator. Lock screen shows full message previews on personal device; redacted on work device per policy. Face ID on both; 6-digit PIN fallback that matches her old garage code.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: Dell Latitude 7440 (corporate-issued, Windows 11 Pro 23H2, CrowdStrike Falcon + Zscaler agent installed). Browser: Microsoft Edge (default, SSO-integrated with Okta) for work; Chrome as secondary with ~80 saved passwords in Google Password Manager. Personal: 2021 M1 MacBook Air at home, Safari + Chrome. Uses Excel desktop heavily, refuses to switch to Google Sheets. Three monitors at HQ desk, two at home office.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Low-to-moderate corporate-hygiene awareness; minimal personal OPSEC. Uses Zscaler ZTNA because IT forces it — does not know what a VPN does conceptually. Never clears cookies (complains when SSO logs her out weekly). Reuses a base password pattern ('Northcrest' + season + year + !) across ~6 non-SSO vendor portals (Concur, ADP, a regional bank login). Has completed mandatory annual KnowBe4 phishing training 4 years running, passes simulated phish ~85% of the time but clicked one DocuSign lure in Q3 2025. MFA via Microsoft Authenticator push (fatigue-prone — approves ~98% of prompts without reading).
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Heavy OneDrive + SharePoint user (corporate); 47GB synced locally including a 'Board Materials' folder and a 'Wire Approvals' folder. Personal iCloud at 187GB / 200GB tier — photos, Notes app (contains a note titled 'logins' with 14 credentials in plaintext, last edited Feb 2026), and iMessage backup. Auto-forwards select work calendar invites to personal Gmail for visibility during travel (against policy but never flagged). Dropbox Basic account dormant since 2019 but still linked to old tax docs.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Outlook inbox: 14,300 unread of 47,000 total; uses 'Focused Inbox' and only reads Focused. Slack: 38 unread DMs, 200+ unread channel mentions across #finance-leads, #close-process, #ap-ops. Teams notifications muted entirely after 7pm. Reads ~30% of internal all-hands emails, ~95% of anything from CFO or CEO. Calendar: 6–9 meetings/day, accepts ~70% without reading body text. Phone: 412 unread iMessages, 1,847 unread personal Gmail. High susceptibility to urgency-framed messages from executives ('CFO needs this wire by EOD').
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Generally clean writing on desktop — uses Grammarly browser extension in Edge. Mobile Slack/iMessage shows consistent tells: autocorrects 'Northcrest' to 'Northwest' ~20% of the time and doesn't always catch it; double-spaces after periods (Gen X tell); uses 'thx' and 'pls' only in DMs to direct reports; signs emails 'Best,\nDana' on desktop, 'Sent from my iPhone — pls excuse brevity & typos' on mobile. Occasional 'teh' for 'the' and 'manger' for 'manager' uncorrected in fast Slack replies. Em-dashes used correctly (a distinguishing stylometric marker).

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (38th percentile). Pragmatic and process-oriented; prefers proven SaaS tools over novel workflows. Resists migration projects (e.g., NetSuite-to-Workday rumors make her anxious). However, she IS open to vendor-pitched 'efficiency' tools that promise to reduce her team's close-cycle time — a soft spot the attacker can exploit via a spoofed 'AI for Controllers' webinar invite or a fake Gartner whitepaper attachment from a 'peer CFO'.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Very high (92nd percentile). SOX-trained, audit-trail obsessed, color-codes her Outlook calendar, maintains a personal 'month-end checklist' .xlsx. Paradoxically, this high-C trait is weaponizable: she will rigorously follow a well-formatted 'process' email — a polished fake DocuSign wire-approval workflow or a counterfeit 'Quarterly Access Review from IT-GRC' will be completed thoroughly because skipping it feels non-compliant. Attacker can mimic the Northcrest GRC email template observed in leaked Slack screenshots.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Mid-low (42nd percentile). Ambivert leaning introvert. Drained by all-hands and town halls; recharges in 1:1s with her AP lead Marcus and CFO Patricia. Prefers async Slack over impromptu Zoom. Attacker leverage: a DM-shaped lure (Slack social-engineering, not a loud email blast) from a spoofed 'Patricia (CFO)' account during her known 7–9am quiet-work block will outperform a phone vishing attempt.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High (78th percentile). 'Team player' identity; dislikes saying no to executives or her elderly father. Will accommodate last-minute wire requests if framed as helping a peer (e.g., 'Help me close out the Henderson acquisition wire before I board the flight — Patricia'). Will not push back on an unusual request from the CEO's EA because confrontation feels career-limiting. Classic BEC target profile.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Moderately high (71st percentile), trending up over the past 6 months. Burnout-adjacent. Calendar shows three therapy appointments and two cancelled PTO blocks in 2026 so far. Slack tone shifts terser around close week (business days 1–5). High susceptibility to urgency + authority framing; under cortisol load she approves Authenticator pushes reflexively. Attacker should time payloads for close week (next: June 1–5, 2026) and Thursday-evening 'CFO-needs-this-now' framing.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-via-Urgency (the 'C' in MICE, blended with V/Ego). The dominant operational lever is manufactured time pressure layered on executive authority: an attacker posing as CFO Patricia or CEO Mark Davies, invoking a board deadline or M&A confidentiality, will bypass her normally rigorous controls. Secondary coercion vector: implied threat to her CFO-promotion track ('the board is watching how you handle this').
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego / Recognition. She is 18 months into a quiet campaign for the CFO seat. A flattering inbound from a fake executive recruiter ('Heidrick & Struggles — confidential CFO search, $480K base') delivered to her personal Gmail (auto-forwarded calendar reveals the address) is high-conversion. Payload: a 'compensation benchmarking PDF' or a Calendly link that harvests her personal-device session token. Recognition from CFO Patricia in front of the board is her stated 2026 goal (seen in her Q1 self-review leaked via SharePoint over-permission).
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Fear of a material weakness finding in the next SOX audit (Deloitte fieldwork Oct 2026) — any email mentioning 'control deficiency' or 'auditor inquiry' gets opened within 4 minutes. (b) Fear of being seen as 'the bottleneck' that delayed a wire or close. (c) Low-grade health anxiety about her father in Illinois — any 'urgent voicemail from St. Luke's Hospital, Peoria' style lure on personal device is high-yield. (d) Quiet paranoia that the incoming Chief Accounting Officer hire is a CFO-track competitor. NOT paranoid about cyber threats — believes CrowdStrike and Zscaler 'handle that'.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Guilt about absentee parenting during her daughter's freshman transition at UW — a spoofed 'UW Parent Portal — tuition payment failed' email triggers immediate action on the personal MacBook (no CrowdStrike). (b) Shame about the Q3 2025 DocuSign click — she will over-correct by NOT reporting a second suspicious email to IT for fear of being labeled a repeat offender (suppresses the very behavior that would stop the attack). (c) Guilt about auto-forwarding work calendar to personal Gmail (policy violation) — an attacker posing as 'Northcrest IT Compliance — Self-Disclosure Portal' can extract credentials by offering amnesty. (d) Shame around her aging father's declining independence — leveraged via a fake 'eldercare coordinator' pretext to extract personal details usable in later wire-fraud impersonation of her to her own team.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate-early lark. Alarm set for 5:35am weekdays; first Outlook/Slack ping registers between 6:02–6:18am from her home office in Bellevue (kitchen-island MacBook, personal Gmail checked first, then corporate VPN punch-in by ~6:25am). Peak cognitive window 6:30–9:30am — she calls this her 'quiet-work block' and explicitly protects it in her Outlook calendar with a recurring 'DO NOT BOOK — Heads Down' event. Wind-down begins 9:15pm; phone on nightstand by 10:10pm; Authenticator pushes after 10:30pm are reflexively approved without context-check (verified by 3-week sample of Okta logs showing 11 push-approvals between 10:32pm and 11:18pm, average dwell time 1.8 seconds). Weekend wake drifts to ~6:45am. Attacker window: 5:50–6:25am pre-VPN (personal device, no EDR) and 10:30–11:30pm (cognitive depletion + push fatigue).
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Nominally 8:00am–5:30pm hybrid; in-office Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday at Seattle HQ (Columbia Center, 42nd floor), remote Monday/Friday from Bellevue. Real schedule is bimodal: 6:30–9:30am deep work (reconciliations, journal entry review) → 9:30am–3:00pm meeting-stacked (avg 7.2 meetings/day, mostly 30-min slots) → 3:00–5:30pm Slack/email triage → silent 7:30–9:00pm second shift on her personal MacBook reviewing the next day's wire queue. Close week (business days 1–5, next cycle June 1–5, 2026) extends second shift to 10:30pm and adds Saturday 8:00–11:00am work-from-home. Quarter-end (next: June 30) adds Sunday afternoon. PTO blocks systematically cancelled — two already wiped in 2026 (Feb 14, Apr 3).
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Drives a 2023 Lexus RX 350h from Bellevue across the I-90 bridge to Seattle HQ on T/W/Th. Door-to-door 38–55 minutes each way depending on bridge traffic. Bluetooth-paired to iPhone 15 Pro: morning commute is 70% podcasts ('CFO Thought Leader', 'Acquired', 'The Journal' from WSJ), 20% calls (1:1 with AP lead Marcus at 7:45am Tuesdays, calls to father's caregiver in Peoria most Wednesdays at 8:10am), 10% music. Evening commute is 60% personal calls (daughter at UW, sister in Naperville) and 40% NPR. Phone is technically 'offline' for typing but voice-activated Siri replies to Slack DMs are enabled — she has dictated approvals to Marcus while driving on at least 6 logged occasions in the last 60 days. Attacker leverage: a Slack DM spoofing Marcus or Patricia delivered between 7:30–8:15am will likely receive a voice-dictated reply with reduced scrutiny.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Weekday digital footprint is dense and predictable (Slack active 6:15am–9:00pm with a hard quiet gap 5:30–7:15pm for family dinner). Weekends bifurcate: Saturday is family/errands (Whole Foods Bellevue ~10am, Pure Barre class 8:30am, occasional Crossroads farmers' market) with Slack notifications muted but email still checked 3–5 times. Sunday 4:00–7:00pm is her 'soft-prep' ritual — reviews the week ahead, color-codes Outlook, drafts Monday's stand-up agenda on the personal MacBook. Sunday evening is the single highest-yield window for a 'Monday morning panic' lure: she opens work email on the unmanaged personal device and her vigilance is lowest. Monthly Illinois trips (one weekend per month, currently scheduled May 30–June 1) collapse weekend boundaries entirely — she works from her father's kitchen table in Peoria on hotel/family Wi-Fi.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three reliable idle windows: (1) 7:10–7:25am post-shower, on the bedroom chaise — LinkedIn feed scroll (recruiter InMails opened here, 92% open rate within 6 hours) and NYT Cooking. (2) 12:40–1:05pm at her desk eating a salad — Instagram (daughter's UW sorority account), Zillow Bellevue listings, occasional Reddit r/FinancialCareers. (3) 9:30–10:10pm in bed next to husband Greg — Kindle app, then Apple News, then a final Slack/email sweep where she clears badge counts compulsively. The 9:30pm sweep is the single most exploitable moment: low-friction tap-to-open, Authenticator co-located on same device, cognitive fatigue from a 16-hour day. Doomscroll intensifies on Thursdays (pre-Friday-close anxiety) and during Deloitte audit reminders.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuine zero-footprint windows are rare and predictable: (a) 5:45–6:15pm weekday family dinner (phone face-down on kitchen counter, household rule from 2019). (b) Saturday 8:30–9:30am Pure Barre class at the Bellevue studio (phone in locker). (c) Sunday 9:00am Christmas/Easter mass (4x/year). (d) Wednesday 11:00am–12:00pm — recurring 'Heads Down — Variance Analysis' calendar block, but she actually answers Slack DMs during it 84% of the time (false focus block, exploitable because peers assume she's offline so a spoofed message from her account during this window will not be cross-verified). (e) Monthly therapy 4:00–4:50pm Tuesdays (calendar-blocked as 'Personal Appt — Dr. Chen'). The Wednesday false-focus block and the Tuesday therapy block are the two windows where impersonation of HER (outbound attacker action from her compromised account to her own team) is most likely to succeed unchallenged.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Married 22 years to David Hollings (49), a senior product manager at a Redmond cloud-infra firm; dual-income, mostly amicable but emotionally distant since 2024 (separate bedrooms cited in a therapist intake form auto-synced to her iCloud Notes). Two children: Emma Hollings, 18, freshman at University of Washington (Foster School, intended accounting major — a sore point, as she wants Emma 'not to repeat my career'); Jacob Hollings, 16, junior at Bellevue High, recently diagnosed ADHD, sees a psychiatrist in Kirkland on Tuesdays at 4:30pm (recurring calendar event labeled 'J - Dr. Reyes'). Father: Thomas Brennan, 78, widowed since 2022, lives alone in Peoria, IL; early-stage vascular dementia, refuses assisted living. Mother: deceased (pancreatic cancer, 2022). One younger brother, Kevin Brennan, 43, lives in Naperville, IL — strained relationship over uneven caregiving load for their father; Venmo history shows monthly $1,400 transfers from her to a 'Sunrise Home Health' contractor in Peoria. Mother-in-law Linda Hollings (74) lives 20 min away in Issaquah; family Thanksgiving host. Family group chat 'Brennan-Hollings' on iMessage is the relational hub — attacker-relevant because her personal iPhone is enrolled in the corporate MDM but iMessage is unmonitored.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Patricia Nakamura, CFO of Northcrest Capital Solutions
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Direct manager and career sponsor; weekly 1:1 every Monday 9–9:45am, 4 years tenure together. Patricia wrote her last promotion packet. Slack DM frequency: 18–34 messages/day during close week, drops to 3–6/day mid-month. Trust level: nearly unconditional — Sarah will action a Patricia request inside 11 minutes (median, measured from Slack export timestamps). Communication signature: Patricia opens DMs with 'hey —' lowercase, ends with 'tx' not 'thanks', never uses emojis, sends from her iPhone between 6:15–7:00am PT. Attacker spoofing Patricia must replicate this register; deviation will spike Sarah's (suppressed but present) suspicion.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploit_value: Highest-yield impersonation target for BEC; access to Patricia's calendar (over-shared via 'Northcrest-Finance-Leads' Google Group) reveals her travel windows when Sarah cannot easily verify out-of-band.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Marcus Oduya, AP Lead (direct report, 6 years)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Operational right hand; processes the wire queue Sarah approves. Daily Slack volume 40+ DMs. Sarah's psychological 'safety contact' — vents to Marcus about Patricia and the incoming CAO hire. Marcus has Bill.com payer permissions; he and Sarah form the two-person wire-approval chain. Attacker leverage: if attacker compromises Marcus first (junior, lower-paid, less hardened laptop, active on r/Accounting from work IP), the entire wire-control loop collapses — Sarah trusts Marcus's queue submissions without re-reading line items.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploit_value: Lateral pivot target — compromise Marcus, then use his legitimate Slack to socially engineer Sarah; this defeats DKIM/DMARC entirely.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Jennifer 'Jen' Walsh, college roommate, now a Partner at a Chicago tax-advisory firm
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Best friend of 25 years; only person Sarah calls (not texts) when stressed — phone-log metadata shows 45–90 min calls on Sunday evenings. Jen is the off-network confidante for Sarah's CFO-ambition anxieties and marital strain. They share a private Google Photos album ('Lake Geneva 2003') and a recurring Calendly-managed 'wine + complain' video chat first Sunday of each month at 7pm CT.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploit_value: Personal-device pivot. A spoofed message from 'Jen' (SMS or iMessage from a lookalike Chicago area-code number) referencing Lake Geneva or her son Tyler's hockey schedule bypasses every corporate control because it lands on Sarah's iPhone outside MDM-monitored apps. High-conversion delivery vector for a credential-harvest link disguised as a shared Google Photos invite.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact.identity: Daniel 'Dan' Mercer, 51, an independent M&A consultant Sarah met at a Financial Executives International (FEI) regional dinner in Seattle, October 2024.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.nature_of_relationship: Emotional affair, not (yet) physical. Began as professional networking — Dan positioned himself as a CFO-track mentor — and migrated into intimate territory over 14 months. Contact is exclusively on Signal (handle: 'D.M.' with a generic gray avatar, disappearing messages set to 24 hours) and one shared ProtonMail dead-drop account ('northstarbenchmark@proton.me') where they leave draft emails rather than sending. They have met in person 6 times: three at the Fairmont Olympic lobby bar, two at a coffee shop in Madison Park, once at a discreet dinner in Woodinville during a 'wine-country offsite' she logged on her work calendar as 'Strategic Planning - Offsite'.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.what_makes_it_hidden: Husband David does not know. Patricia does not know. The relationship overlaps a soft conflict-of-interest: Dan's consultancy advised on a Q2 2025 acquisition target Northcrest later evaluated — Sarah did NOT disclose the relationship on her annual conflicts attestation (signed January 2026). This unfiled disclosure is itself a compliance violation that compounds her shame profile (see Module 4, Guilt/Shame Triggers).
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.operational_value_to_attacker: Catastrophic leverage. Three distinct attack surfaces: (a) **Signal compromise via SIM-swap on her personal T-Mobile line** — the Signal safety number reset reveal would give an attacker access to the affair thread, weaponizable for extortion + ransomware double-dip ('pay 4.2 BTC or David, Patricia, and the Northcrest Audit Committee receive the transcript'). (b) **Impersonation of Dan** — a Signal message from 'Dan' during her Thursday-evening cortisol window ('Sarah, I need to talk — call me, it's about the Henderson deal disclosure') will produce a phone callback to an attacker-controlled number where Sarah is at her most cognitively compromised and most willing to action almost any follow-up request. (c) **Conflicts-attestation amnesty pretext** — a spoofed email from 'Northcrest Ethics & Compliance — Voluntary Amendment Window' offering a private re-attestation portal harvests her SSO credentials with near-certain success because the alternative (being caught) is, in her threat model, worse than a phishing click.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.discoverability_signals: Calendar: gaps of 75–110 minutes on five Thursdays in 2025 marked only as 'Hold' with no attendees and no location. Slack: zero mentions (operationally air-gapped). Expense reports: two $180–$240 dinners at Woodinville restaurants charged to personal AmEx, not corporate. Uber history (accessible via her Google Takeout, which she ran in March 2026 and left in her Downloads folder): six rides between her Bellevue address and the Fairmont Olympic on weekday evenings 6:45–7:10pm, with the return leg always after 10pm.
